questions.explanation: The HCFA-1500, now called the CMS-1500, is the standard paper claim form used by individual providers and suppliers to bill Medicare, Medicaid, and other third-party payers for professional services. The UB-04 is used by institutional facilities such as hospitals.

questions.stem: On a health insurance claim form, which coding system is used to report the diagnosis or reason a patient sought care?
questions.explanation: ICD (International Classification of Diseases) codes identify the patient's diagnosis, condition, or reason for the encounter. CPT codes, by contrast, describe the procedures and services that were performed.

questions.stem: Which type of equipment is considered nonexpendable (capital) and is typically inventoried and depreciated over multiple years?
questions.explanation: Nonexpendable or capital equipment, such as an isokinetic dynamometer, has a lasting service life, is inventoried, and is depreciated over several years. Expendable supplies like tape, wraps, and applicators are consumed and reordered regularly.

questions.stem: Which legal element must be proven to establish negligence, referring to the level of care an ordinarily prudent professional would provide under similar circumstances?
questions.explanation: The standard of care is the degree of care a reasonably prudent professional with similar training would exercise under the same circumstances. A breach of this standard that causes harm is a central element in proving negligence.

questions.stem: Which organization is responsible for certifying athletic trainers and administering the entry-level certification examination in the United States?
questions.explanation: The Board of Certification (BOC) develops and administers the certification examination and establishes standards for entry-level athletic trainers. The NATA is the professional membership association, and CAATE accredits education programs.

questions.stem: True or False: Evidence-based practice integrates the best available research evidence with clinical expertise and the patient's values and circumstances.
questions.explanation: Evidence-based practice is defined as the integration of the best available research evidence, the clinician's expertise, and the individual patient's values and preferences to guide clinical decision-making.

questions.stem: True or False: Under FERPA, a student-athlete's educational records may be freely shared with any coach or booster without the student's written consent.
questions.explanation: The Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act (FERPA) protects the privacy of student education records and generally requires written consent from the eligible student before personally identifiable information is disclosed to third parties.

questions.stem: What is the correct chronological order of these steps in the human resources employment cycle? I. Performance evaluation II. Recruitment III. Termination IV. Orientation V. Selection/Hiring
questions.explanation: The logical HR employment cycle begins with recruitment (II), followed by selection/hiring (V), then orientation of the new employee (IV), ongoing performance evaluation (I), and finally termination (III) at the end of employment.

questions.stem: An athletic trainer instructs an untrained student manager to fit and dispense a prophylactic knee brace to an athlete without any supervision or documentation. The athlete is later injured because the brace was improperly fitted. In this scenario, which legal concept is MOST directly implicated by the athletic trainer's decision to delegate this task?
options: Negligence through improper delegation and failure to supervise
Assumption of risk by the athlete
correct_answer: Negligence through improper delegation and failure to supervise
questions.explanation: Delegating a clinical task to an unqualified, unsupervised individual breaches the standard of care and can constitute negligence when it results in foreseeable harm. The athletic trainer retains responsibility for adequately supervising delegated duties.

questions.stem: Which of the following are components of a legally sound informed consent for treatment? (Select all that apply.)
options: Explanation of the nature of the proposed treatment
Disclosure of the risks and benefits involved
A guarantee that the treatment will be successful
Voluntary agreement given by a competent individual
correct_answer: Explanation of the nature of the proposed treatment
Disclosure of the risks and benefits involved
Voluntary agreement given by a competent individual
questions.explanation: Valid informed consent requires explaining the nature of the treatment, disclosing associated risks and benefits, describing reasonable alternatives, and obtaining a voluntary agreement from a competent person. A guarantee of successful outcome is never a legitimate component of informed consent.
